text: you know there are times admittedly very

insufferable actors and Relentless

watched something like the electric
State a bloated Meandering tedious

that doesn't know what it wants to be or
what sort of story it wants to tell
that's weighed down by lazy phoned in
performances from its normally talented

million to
produce and I realized that nah it's
pretty much business as usual the
electric state is a film that has
absolutely no reason to exist that has
no Pace or energy or dramatic momentum
no idea what to do with its bloated cast
of characters and that pretty much
represents all the worst excesses of an
industry that's got too much money too

drive or simple common sense to know
what to do with them it's actually kind
of weird to go from reviewing something
like Firefly that did a lot with very
little to the electric state which does
almost nothing with a great deal anyway

State what a piece of [ __ ] honestly
After experiencing the two hours of
self-inflicted suffering that is this
movie I was genuinely angry by the time
it was over not only because I'd sunk a
128 minutes of my life into something so
completely boring and forgettable that I
was struggling to remember what was even
going on while it was happening but
because I couldn't help thinking about
all the other stuff that could have
gotten made instead of this complete
anal EXC crucia for the price of this
one movie you could have financed 21
Godzilla minus 1's or 10 seasons of
firefly you could make Terminator 2
three times over and still have money to
spare and it's not like every penny of
that ridiculous budget is up on screen
either the CGI is passable at best and
dog [ __ ] at worst there wasn't a single
moment of spectacle that even vaguely
impressed me and even the action set
pieces are depressingly bloodless and
unimaginative movie like the Creator
were made on a budget of $80 million and
somehow looked way better than this
turds I don't know maybe they spent all
the money on their cast which on paper
is impressive at least getting Chris
Pratt Jean Carlo Esposito Stanley Tucci
Woody harlson Anthony Macky and Brian
Cox all together in one movie is pretty
impressive but they're all just playing
the same stock character as they've done
a million times before with about as
much depth as the puddle of piss that
had formed under my chair by the time
the film was over and you can almost
feel the I'm radiating out through the

phoning it in for a quick and easy
payday and honestly I wouldn't be
surprised if that's what actually
happens K hewan is the only one who
actually seems to be trying here and as
much as I love the fact that he's making

character off [ __ ] off film Brown and
Pratt have got absolutely no chemistry
together probably cuz the script seems a

from them there's too much of an age gap
for a romantic relationship Pratt's
character is too immature for a
fatherdaughter kind of connection and
they're too different for a generic
friendship so like everything else in
this movie they just kind of meander
along with no idea what they're supposed
to be doing even the direction is
disappointingly apathetic like the Russo
brothers were coerced into making this
film and vowed to put as little effort
into it as possible I've heard people
try to make the argument that this film
was written as a goofy comedy Caper that
you're not supposed to think too much
about which is a [ __ ] argument in
the first place because even comedies
can make sense and second anyone who's
actually watched the film would know
that it absolutely wants you to take its
characters and Stakes seriously the
electric State wants to be seen as some
beautiful tragic fairy tale about family
bonds the power of love and sacrifice
the nature of humanity and Consciousness
and the ethical questions that arise
when you create those things with
technology but it's kind of difficult to
buy into Grand philosophical ideas like
that when you're watching Mr peanut head
having a sword fight against Jan Carlo
Esposito in a mech suit yeah I can
definitely smell
shite ultimately the electric state is a
movie that left me genuinely baffled by
its very existence it has the budget and
star power of a major Hollywood
Blockbuster built around a premise that
should have been a low-budget sci-fi
drama and shed out onto a streaming
service where it can never possibly hope
to recoup its massive costs is it
supposed to be some weird money
laundering scheme do Netflix just hate
making a profit I don't [ __ ] know but
I do know one thing you should save
yourself 2 hours of misery and go watch
literally anything else now if you'll
